name: [BUG-GW-002] TC-GW-003 — OPTIONS preflight must not allow arbitrary origins (e.g. https://evil.com)
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-002]**

**What this test does:**
Sends an OPTIONS (CORS preflight) request to /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status with "Origin: https://evil.com" to test whether the gateway accepts arbitrary origins.

**Expected:** The server should NOT reflect "https://evil.com" in the access-control-allow-origin header. It should either return the trusted origin or omit the header entirely.

**Actual:** HTTP 200 with access-control-allow-origin: * — meaning "https://evil.com" is implicitly allowed (wildcard accepts all origins)

**Why this matters:**
CORS preflights are the browser's way of asking "is this origin allowed to make this cross-origin request?" By answering "*", the gateway tells the browser to allow ALL origins, including malicious ones. This negates CORS as a security mechanism entirely.

**Fix:** Replace the wildcard CORS plugin with an explicit origin allowlist in APISIX. The OPTIONS response should only reflect the requesting origin if it is in the allowlist.
descriptionHtml:
[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-002]

What this test does:
Sends an OPTIONS (CORS preflight) request to /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status with "Origin: https://evil.com" to test whether the gateway accepts arbitrary origins.

Expected: The server should NOT reflect "https://evil.com" in the access-control-allow-origin header. It should either return the trusted origin or omit the header entirely.

Actual: HTTP 200 with access-control-allow-origin: * — meaning "https://evil.com" is implicitly allowed (wildcard accepts all origins)

Why this matters:
CORS preflights are the browser's way of asking "is this origin allowed to make this cross-origin request?" By answering "*", the gateway tells the browser to allow ALL origins, including malicious ones. This negates CORS as a security mechanism entirely.

Fix: Replace the wildcard CORS plugin with an explicit origin allowlist in APISIX. The OPTIONS response should only reflect the requesting origin if it is in the allowlist.

testStage.description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-GW-002]**

**What this test does:**
Sends an OPTIONS (CORS preflight) request to /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status with "Origin: https://evil.com" to test whether the gateway accepts arbitrary origins.

**Expected:** The server should NOT reflect "https://evil.com" in the access-control-allow-origin header. It should either return the trusted origin or omit the header entirely.

**Actual:** HTTP 200 with access-control-allow-origin: * — meaning "https://evil.com" is implicitly allowed (wildcard accepts all origins)

**Why this matters:**
CORS preflights are the browser's way of asking "is this origin allowed to make this cross-origin request?" By answering "*", the gateway tells the browser to allow ALL origins, including malicious ones. This negates CORS as a security mechanism entirely.

**Fix:** Replace the wildcard CORS plugin with an explicit origin allowlist in APISIX. The OPTIONS response should only reflect the requesting origin if it is in the allowlist.
